title: Return Rate by Category

landmines.name: returns-fan-out
landmines.kills: Joining order_items to returns and summing quantity multiplies the sold quantity by the number of return rows on a line (partial returns), corrupting the denominator. Kills naive/1_fanout_intdiv_innerjoin.sql.

landmines.name: integer-division
landmines.kills: SUM(quantity_returned) / SUM(quantity) is INTEGER/INTEGER and truncates every real rate below 1 to 0. Kills naive/1_fanout_intdiv_innerjoin.sql on integer-division engines (sqlite, postgres, duckdb).

landmines.name: empty-group-dropped
landmines.kills: An INNER JOIN to returns deletes categories that sold something but were never returned; they must appear with return_rate 0. Kills naive/1_fanout_intdiv_innerjoin.sql and naive/2_innerjoin_drops_zero_return.sql.

notes: Sold and returned units are aggregated on separate grains and combined with a LEFT JOIN, so partial-return fan-out never touches the denominator. Real division via 1.0* is portable and avoids MySQL/SQLite divergence on integer division. MSSQL: 1.0* division and ROUND behave the same.
